2026-04-24T02:11:15.3036069Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-24T02:11:15.3036628Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-24T02:11:15.3037050Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-24T02:11:15.3037409Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-24T02:11:15.3037723Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-24T02:11:15.3037956Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-24T02:11:15.3038112Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-24T02:11:15.3038290Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-24T02:11:15.3038456Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-24T02:11:15.3038684Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-24T02:11:15.3038884Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-24T02:11:15.3039163Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-24T02:11:15.3039415Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. 2026-04-24T02:13:34.9746463Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-24T02:12:17.243296271Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":10,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":2944135197,\"number\":49062,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":true,\"title\":\"[FEATURE REQ] ServiceBusTrigger - Options to manage backpressure / consumption rate\",\"body\":\"### Library name\\n\\nMicrosoft.Azure.WebJobs.Extensions.ServiceBus\\n\\n### Please describe the feature.\\n\\n**Problem:**\\nIf a Function App is consuming a queue or a topic with a **ServiceBusTrigger** to make REST API calls to a slower service that have a rate limiter (or any other slower operation), this function will suffer backpressure without being able to control/define the rate of message consumption. The problem is even more tricky when considering the Function App horizontal scaling.\\n\\n**Feature request:**\\nThe perfect solution would be to have ServiceBusTrigger parameters that allow to define the expected consumption rate over time, to fully support the [queue based load leveling pattern](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/patterns/queue-based-load-leveling) and **control** the processing cadence.\\n\\nAnother option can be that the **ServiceBusMessageActions** allows us to inform that the message must be retried later so that we can propagate the \\\"Retry-After\\\" information received from targeted service or any other computed time. Ideally, it should not increment the number of tries, as it was not an \\\"error\\\". \\nSomthing like:\\n`DelayMessageAsync(DateTime retryAfter, bool incrementRetries)`\\n\\n**What we already tried as workaround:**\\n- **maxConcurrentCalls + delays:** Problem is that the **maxConcurrentCalls** is applied per instance, making the solution unpredictable at scale. The scaling can be blocked to 1 instance, but it makes the solution not viable if a single instance cannot reach the targeted service limits.\\n- **Message scheduling:** We tested a potential solution by re-queuing the message with scheduled time based on Retry-After received from targeted service. On paper, it can look great. But if the Function App is paused for some reason, when restarting, it floods the service before re-scheduling, and in our case, unfortunately, the service rate limiting was quite aggressive and blacklisting in such case.\\n- **TimerTrigger:** By using a **TimerTrigger** and reading messages with a **ServiceBusReceiver** we were able to create a processing cadence. But this solution is also suffering scaling issue because the trigger is only executed by one single instance, meaning that the solution is not viable if a single instance cannot reach the targeted service limits.\\n\\n**Note:**\\nIn this backpressure scenario, it must be considered that the global processing rate over time allows the queue/topic to not permanently grow.\\n\",\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"foliv57\",\"id\":59969260,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjU5OTY5MjYw\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/59969260?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/foliv57\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/foliv57\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/foliv57/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/foliv57/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/foliv57/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/foliv57/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/foliv57/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/foliv57/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/foliv57/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/foliv57/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/foliv57/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1178447860,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Client\",\"name\":\"Client\",\"color\":\"ffeb77\",\"description\":\"This issue is related to a non-management package\",\"default\":false,\"node_id\":\"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ3ODYw\"},{\"id\":1353316183,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/customer-reported\",\"name\":\"customer-reported\",\"color\":\"3800e0\",\"description\":\"Issues that are reported by GitHub users external to the Azure organization.\",\"default\":false,\"node_id\":\"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzUzMzE2MTgz\"},{\"id\":1369687945,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/question\",\"name\":\"question\",\"color\":\"eaa875\",\"description\":\"The REQ] ServiceBusMessageActions - Add ScheduleMessageAsync method\",\"body\":\"### Library name\\n\\nMicrosoft.Azure.Functions.Worker\\n\\n### Please describe the feature.\\n\\nTo implement retry logic like an exponential backoff retry policy, it would be great to have ScheduleMessageAsync to return the message to the service bus and increment the delivery count instead of having to create a new message and add an Application Property to manage the retries.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe expected code should be:\\r\\n```csharp\\r\\npublic Task ScheduleMessageAsync(ServiceBusReceivedMessage message, ServiceBusMessageActions actions, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\r\\n{\\r\\n    if (retryCount \\u003e message.DeliveryCount)\\r\\n    {\\r\\n        return actions.DeadLetterMessageAsync(message, null, \\\"MAX_RETRIES\\\", \\\"Max retry count reached\\\", cancellationToken);\\r\\n    }\\r\\n\\r\\n    DateTimeOffset now = DateTimeOffset.Now;\\r\\n    DateTimeOffset possibleDelay = now.Add(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(Math.Pow(3, retryCount) + 10));\\r\\n    DateTimeOffset maxDelay = now.Add(_maxDelay);\\r\\n    DateTimeOffset scheduledDatetime = possibleDelay \\u003c maxDelay ? possibleDelay : maxDelay;\\r\\n\\r\\n    return actions.ScheduleMessageAsync(message, scheduledDatetime, cancellationToken);\\r\\n}\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nCurrently you need to do something like this:\\r\\n```csharp\\r\\npublic Task ScheduleMessageAsync(ServiceBusReceivedMessage message, ServiceBusMessageActions actions, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\r\\n{\\r\\n    object? retryCountProperty = message.ApplicationProperties.GetValueOrDefault(RETRY_PROPERTY);\\r\\n    int retryCount = Int32.Parse(retryCountProperty?.ToString() ?? \\\"0\\\");\\r\\n\\r\\n    if (retryCount \\u003e _maxRetries)\\r\\n    {\\r\\n        return actions.DeadLetterMessageAsync(message, null, \\\"MAX_RETRIES\\\", \\\"Max retry count reached\\\", cancellationToken);\\r\\n    }\\r\\n\\r\\n    DateTimeOffset now = DateTimeOffset.Now;\\r\\n    DateTimeOffset possibleDelay = now.Add(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(Math.Pow(3, retryCount) + 10));\\r\\n    DateTimeOffset maxDelay = now.Add(_maxDelay);\\r\\n    DateTimeOffset scheduledDatetime = possibleDelay \\u003c maxDelay ? possibleDelay : maxDelay;\\r\\n\\r\\n    ServiceBusSender sender = _client.CreateSender(_queueName);\\r\\n    ServiceBusMessage retryMessage = new(message);\\r\\n    retryMessage.ApplicationProperties.Remove(RETRY_PROPERTY);\\r\\n    retryMessage.ApplicationProperties.Add(RETRY_PROPERTY, ++retryCount);\\r\\n\\r\\n    return sender.ScheduleMessageAsync(retryMessage, scheduledDatetime, cancellationToken);\\r\\n}\\r\\n```\\r\\n\",\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"adearriba\",\"id\":4951003,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjQ5NTEwMDM=\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4951003?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/adearriba\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/adearriba\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/adearriba/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/adearriba/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/adearriba/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/adearriba/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/adearriba/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/adearriba/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/adearriba/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/adearriba/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/adearriba/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":134456219,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Service%20Bus\",\"name\":\"Service MessageLockLostException in Microsoft.Azure.ServiceBus\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\r\\n\\r\\nMicrosoft.Azure.ServiceBus 4.2.1\\r\\n\\r\\n### Describe the bug\\r\\n\\r\\nI am deploying function app with service bus trigger. The function app has multiple functions listening with different subscriptions to same topic. Before deploying the function app to production slot, we disable the subscription using az command as the message needs to be processed only once as it does registration of user. \\r\\n\\r\\n`az servicebus topic subscription update --resource-group xxx --namespace-name yyy --topic-name mytopic --name subscription --status \\\"ReceiveDisabled\\\"`\\r\\n\\r\\nAfter the app is deployed we enable the subscription\\r\\n\\r\\n`az servicebus topic subscription update --resource-group xxx  --namespace-name yyy  --topic-name mytopic  --name subscription  --status \\\"Active\\\"`\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\n### Expected behavior\\r\\n\\r\\n\\r\\nWe expect all the message in the queue to be processed \\r\\n\\r\\n### Actual behavior\\r\\n\\r\\nRoughly half the message throw following error message when we are trying to Complete the message\\r\\n\\r\\n**Exception while executing function: CartaDispatcherSendRegistration The lock supplied is invalid. Either the lock expired, or the message has already been removed from the queue, or was received by a different receiver** \\r\\n\\r\\nThe subscription is set to following\\r\\nlockout dution : 1m 15 mins\\r\\nAutoComplete: Off\\r\\nmaxAutoRenewDuration 5 mins\\r\\nmaxConcurrentCalls: 16 (default)\\r\\nMax delivery count: 1\\r\\n\\r\\nNow what is interesting is this error only occurs when I disable and enable Subscription. If I stop the function and let the queue build up and then  enable the function, there is no error. \\r\\n\\r\\nIf I reduce maxConcurrentCalls down to 1, then it works fine with no errors but this has a significant impact on performance\\r\\n\\r\\n### Reproduction Steps\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Create a very simple app listening to a topic and subscription\\r\\n `using System;\\r\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\r\\nusing Microsoft.Azure.ServiceBus.Core;\\r\\nusing Microsoft.Azure.WebJobs;\\r\\nusing Microsoft.Azure.WebJobs.Host;\\r\\nusing Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;\\r\\n\\r\\nnamespace ServiceBus\\r\\n{\\r\\n    public class Function1\\r\\n    {\\r\\n        private readonly ILogger\\u003cFunction1\\u003e _logger;\\r\\n\\r\\n        public Function1(ILogger\\u003cFunction1\\u003e log)\\r\\n        {\\r\\n            _logger = log;\\r\\n        }\\r\\n\\r\\n        [FunctionName(\\\"Function1\\\")]\\r\\n        public async Task Run([ServiceBusTrigger(\\\"partition-topic\\\", \\\"hello-world\\\", Connection  = \\\"AzureServiceBus\\\")]string mySbMsg, string lockToken, MessageReceiver messageReceiver,  ILogger logger)\\r\\n        {\\r\\n            messageReceiver.PrefetchCount = 0;\\r\\n            messageReceiver.\\r\\n            _logger.LogInformation($\\\"C# ServiceBus topic trigger function processed message: {mySbMsg}\\\");\\r\\n            Console.WriteLine(mySbMsg);\\r\\n            try\\r\\n            {\\r\\n                await messageReceiver.CompleteAsync(lockToken);\\r\\n            }\\r\\n            catch (Exception ex)\\r\\n            {\\r\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\\"Error: {ex}\\\");\\r\\n                await messageReceiver.DeadLetterAsync(lockToken);\\r\\n            }\\r\\n\\r\\n        }\\r\\n    }\\r\\n}\\r\\n`\\r\\n2. Start the function app\\r\\n3. Set the Subscription to ReceiveDisabled with use az or portal\\r\\n4. Send some 100 test messages\\r\\n5. Set the Subscription to Enable\\r\\n6. See the app throw errors\\r\\n\\r\\nI am using .Net 6\\r\\n\\r\\n### Environment\\r\\n\\r\\n.NET SDK:\\r\\n Version:   7.0.306\\r\\n Commit:    f500069cb7\\r\\n\\r\\nRuntime Environment:\\r\\n OS Name:     Windows\\r\\n OS Version:  10.0.19045\\r\\n OS Platform: Windows\\r\\n RID:         win10-x64\\r\\n Base Path:   C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk\\\\7.0.306\\\\\\r\\n\\r\\nHost:\\r\\n  Version:      7.0.9\\r\\n  Architecture: x64\\r\\n  Commit:       8e9a17b221\\r\\n.NET SDKs installed:\\r\\n  3.1.426 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\r\\n  5.0.408 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\r\\n  5.0.414 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\r\\n  6.0.120 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\r\\n  6.0.315 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\r\\n  7.0.306 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\r\\n\\r\\n.NET runtimes installed:\\r\\n  Microsoft.AspNetCore.App 3.1.25 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\shared\\\\Microsoft.AspNetCore.App]\\r\\n  Microsoft.AspNetCore.App 3.1.31 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\shared\\\\Microsoft.AspNetCore.App]\\r\\n  Microsoft.AspNetCore.App 3.1.32 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\shared\\\\Microsoft.AspNetCore.App]\\r\\n  Microsoft.AspNetCore.App 5.0.17 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\shared\\\\Microsoft.AspNetCore.App]\\r\\n  Microsoft.AspNetCore.App 6.0.5 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\shared\\\\Microsoft.AspNetCore.App]\\r\\n  Microsoft.AspNetCore.App 6.0.11 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\shared\\\\Microsoft.AspNetCore.App]\\r\\n  Microsoft.AspNetCore.App 6.0.20 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\shared\\\\Microsoft.AspNetCore.App]\\r\\n  Microsoft.AspNetCore.App 7.0.9 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\shared\\\\Microsoft.AspNetCore.App]\\r\\n  Microsoft.NETCore.App 3.1.25 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\shared\\\\Microsoft.NETCore.App]\\r\\n  Microsoft.NETCore.App 3.1.31 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\shared\\\\Microsoft.NETCore.App]
